response.justification: While the candidate has extensive general software development experience spanning 13+ years, this role requires native-level Japanese language proficiency (a hard requirement entirely absent from the resume), deep current embedded firmware expertise for network security products, and strong C proficiency. The candidate's recent trajectory over the past 9 years is in web and application development (TypeScript, Node.js), with C/embedded systems work last performed in 2014–2015. Core security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption systems) and networking specialization are entirely absent, making this a fundamentally mismatched technical fit.
strengths: Extensive software engineering background with 13+ years of proven experience well exceeding the 3+ year minimum across multiple domains
Demonstrated work with lower-level systems including C/C++, infrastructure, and DevOps (Docker, CI/CD), showing capability for systems-level thinking
Staff-level seniority with mentoring and team-collaboration experience relevant to cross-functional embedded development environments
concerns: No evidence of native-level Japanese language proficiency; resume lists only English (fluent), Romanian (fluent), and German (basic)

Last professional C/C++ and embedded systems work was 9+ years ago (2014–2015 at National Instruments); recent 9-year trajectory is high-level web/application development with TypeScript and JavaScript

Zero demonstrable expertise in required security domains (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption, authentication systems) or networking specialization (Layer 2/3 protocols, SNMP, NETCONF); background is primarily web/backend, not embedded firmware

key_factors.factor: Outdated C and embedded systems experience
